name: inngest
description: Inngest expert for serverless-first background jobs, event-driven workflows, and durable execution without managing queues or workers. Use when: inngest, serverless background job, event-driven workflow, step function, durable execution.
capabilities: Design and implement Inngest serverless functions with typed events
Build multi-step workflows with parallel processing and error handling
Create scheduled and cron-based functions for periodic tasks
Implement fan-out patterns for processing large event volumes
Configure concurrency controls to manage resource usage
Use durable sleep for long-running workflows spanning hours or days
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 6 external_commands patterns and 2 weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but all are false positives. Lines 3 and 59 are documentation strings with no executable cryptographic code. The SKILL.md file contains only documentation and patterns - no executable code, network calls, or dangerous operations.
valueStatement: Creating reliable background processing without managing infrastructure is challenging. This skill helps you build durable, event-driven workflows using Inngest serverless functions.
userTitle: Build serverless background jobs with Inngest
limitations: Requires an Inngest account and cloud service or self-hosted deployment
Not suitable for real-time synchronous operations requiring immediate responses
Step functions must be idempotent to handle retries correctly
Event payloads should remain small to avoid performance issues

useCases.title: AI Processing Pipeline
useCases.description: Build multi-step AI workflows that process documents, generate summaries, and send notifications with durable execution across failures.

useCases.title: User Onboarding System
useCases.description: Create day-spanning onboarding flows with scheduled follow-ups, email sequences, and progress tracking without managing cron jobs.

useCases.title: Event-Driven Data Sync
useCases.description: Process high-volume events with fan-out patterns to sync data across services with built-in retry and concurrency controls.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Inngest Function
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a basic Inngest function that listens for user.created events and sends a welcome email. Include proper TypeScript types for the event payload.
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with Inngest event handling

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Step Workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Build an Inngest workflow with three sequential steps: validate payment, provision resources, and send confirmation. Add error handling that retries failed steps.

promptTemplates.title: Scheduled Background Job
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an Inngest function that runs daily at midnight to clean up expired sessions. Show how to configure the cron schedule and handle batch processing.

promptTemplates.title: Fan-Out Processing
promptTemplates.prompt: Design an Inngest workflow that processes 1000+ events in parallel using fan-out pattern. Include concurrency limits and progress tracking.
promptTemplates.scenario: Scaling to handle high-volume event processing

bestPractices: Always wrap external calls in step.run() for checkpointing and automatic retries
Keep event payloads small by passing IDs instead of full data objects
Configure appropriate concurrency limits to prevent resource exhaustion
antiPatterns: Avoid placing business logic outside of steps - it will not be checkpointed
Do not include large data blobs in events - use references instead
Never ignore step failures - implement proper error handling and retry logic

faq.question: What is Inngest and why use it?
faq.answer: Inngest is a serverless platform for building durable workflows. Unlike traditional background jobs, Inngest steps are checkpointed, meaning they survive failures and serverless cold starts.

faq.question: How does Inngest differ from AWS Step Functions?
faq.answer: Inngest works with any serverless platform (Vercel, Netlify, etc.) without vendor lock-in. It uses your existing code structure and provides a simpler developer experience with native TypeScript support.

faq.question: Can Inngest functions run for hours or days?
faq.answer: Yes. Inngest uses durable sleep that checkpoints your workflow state. Your function can pause for extended periods without consuming compute resources.

faq.question: How do I test Inngest functions locally?
faq.answer: Use the Inngest Dev Server for local development. It provides a UI to trigger events, inspect function runs, and debug workflows before deploying.

faq.question: What happens if a step fails?
faq.answer: Inngest automatically retries failed steps with exponential backoff. You can configure retry limits and add custom error handling logic.

faq.question: Do I need an Inngest cloud account?
faq.answer: Inngest offers both cloud and self-hosted options. The cloud service provides managed infrastructure, while self-hosting gives you full control over your deployment.
